rules.rule: No compat shims, wrapper modules, aliases for retired names, migration helpers, or anticipatory code. A rename replaces every call site in the same change.

Extend the canonical owner: each vocabulary axis (color, keybind, MCP server, tool admission) has exactly one declaring owner per docs/standards/nix-doctrine.md, and new capability lands as a row on that owner. A new singleton file or duplicated value for an owned axis is a defect.

Shell bodies — packaged via writeShellApplication or standalone .sh including .claude hooks — declare runtimeInputs where packaged, use set -euo pipefail, pass shellcheck without directives, and embed no repo paths, usernames, or magic values. An '|| true' survives only as a pipefail rail where non-zero exit is an expected data condition (probe no-match, vanished process, torn tail line of a live-appended file); an '|| true' that hides a real failure class is a defect.

forge-provision emits schema-v3 JSON envelopes only; agent-facing JSON never carries socket paths, host absolute paths, or token material; every envelope change lands with its README contract row; jq programs live in jq/ files, never inlined in Bash.

services/ TypeScript follows docs/stacks/typescript law: Effect rails, tagged faults, Config.redacted, Schema boundary decodes, typed Pulumi rows, secrets never in unredacted state outputs. Dependency movement is forward-only: a downgrade or stale pin without a recorded reason is a defect.

Lua and TOML app config consumes Nix-owned vocabulary projections; a private hex color, ad hoc keybind, or duplicated theme value in consumer config is a defect — the value belongs on the generating owner.

Cross-module seams are single-owner projections mapped in docs/atlas/interconnection.md. A change touching an owner surface (theme roles, chord rows, ssh host rows, ignoreEstate, MCP fleet manifest, register grammar, receipt env-key grammar, attention-feed fields, XDG projection paths, zjstatus pipe names) must land with its consumers verified; a consumer-side literal that re-derives an owner value, a renamed field only one side knows about, or a new kernel hand-rolling an owned grammar (receipt paths, launchd labels, envelope keys) is drift.

Workflow scripts are deterministic orchestration: schema-typed agent outputs, bounded loops, stage products written to disk with path receipts. Bare promises, unbounded polling, wall-clock branching, and subagents instructed to wait are defects.
